document.title: Periodic Safety Update Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: IMDRF Adverse Event Terminology Annex A
IMDRF Adverse Event Terminology Annex C
IMDRF Adverse Event Terminology Annex D
IMDRF Adverse Event Terminology Annex F

sourceSummary.oneLine: PSUR document template for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models under EU MDR post-market surveillance requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports preparation of a Periodic Safety Update Report under Article 86 of Regulation (EU) 2017/745, including PMS, vigilance, CAPA, PMCF, sales, population exposure, and PSUR update period information.
keyPoints: Manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. with SRN KR-MF-000020684.
Notified Body listed as 3EC International a.s. (2265), with certificate ID noted as in progress of conformity assessment.
The device is identified as Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, Class IIa, models A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW.
Class IIa PSUR update period is stated as once in two years.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: Regulation (EU) 2017/745
entities.evidence: Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council

concepts.name: Periodic Safety Update Report
concepts.evidence: Manufacturers of class IIa, class IIb, and class III devices shall prepare a periodic safety update report in accordance with the article 86 of the Regulation (EU) 2017/745 (MDR).

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance data
concepts.evidence: summarize results and conclusions of the analyses of the post-market surveillance data gathered as a result of the post-market surveillance plan

concepts.name: Vigilance data
concepts.evidence: Vigilance data consist of information concerning serious incidents, field safety corrective actions (FSCAs) and trend reports.

concepts.name: Corrective and Preventive Actions
concepts.evidence: Provide a list of all corrective and / or preventive actions (CAPA) according to Article 83(4) and to Article 86.

concepts.name: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: The sections below should be completed in alignment with the PMS and PMCF plans.

concepts.name: Summary of Safety and Clinical Performance applicability
concepts.pageTarget: Summary of Safety and Clinical Performance
concepts.evidence: N/A - The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) are not applicable for SSCP. It is a class IIa device.

concepts.name: Class IIa PSUR update period
concepts.pageTarget: EU MDR Class IIa PSUR Update Period
concepts.evidence: Manufacturers of class IIa devices shall update the PSUR when necessary and at least every two years.

evidence.claim: Under MDR Article 86, manufacturers of class IIa, class IIb, and class III devices shall prepare a PSUR.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturers of class IIa, class IIb, and class III devices shall prepare a periodic safety update report in accordance with the article 86 of the Regulation (EU) 2017/745 (MDR).

evidence.claim: The report summarizes PMS data analyses and preventive and corrective actions for the device.
evidence.evidenceText: summarize results and conclusions of the analyses of the post-market surveillance data gathered as a result of the post-market surveillance plan... together with a rationale and description of any preventive and corrective actions taken.

evidence.claim: For class III or implantable devices, PSURs are submitted through the MDR Article 92 electronic system to the notified body.
evidence.evidenceText: For class III devices or implantable devices, manufacturers shall submit PSURs by means of the electronic system referred to in Article 92 of MDR to the notified body involved in the conformity assessment

evidence.claim: For other devices, manufacturers make PSURs available to the notified body and upon request to competent authorities.
evidence.evidenceText: For devices other than those, manufacturers shall make PSURs available to the notified body involved in the conformity assessment and, upon request, to competent authorities.

evidence.claim: Vigilance data include serious incidents, FSCAs, and trend reports.
evidence.evidenceText: Vigilance data consist of information concerning serious incidents, field safety corrective actions (FSCAs) and trend reports.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not provided; the source shows only the placeholder 'Rev. Date 날짜' and 'Next PSUR will be conducted on month 20XX.'
Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but source text identifies EU MDR, EUDAMED, Notified Body, EEA, Turkey, and Northern Ireland regulatory context.
The document appears to be a template or draft with placeholders such as PSUR-year, year.01.01 ~ year.12.31, blank approval signatures, and many empty tables.
Certificate ID is not final; source states it is 'in the progress of conformity assessment.'
